description: Extend PipelineRuntime to support ServiceInput plugins (start/stop lifecycle)
steps: Add `isServiceInput()` type guard to src/core/plugin-types.ts
Add `metric_batch_size` support to output flush loop (split large batches)
Modify PipelineRuntime.start() to detect ServiceInput vs Input
For ServiceInput: call start(acc) instead of creating gather loop
For ServiceInput: store reference for shutdown
Modify PipelineRuntime.stop() to call stop() on all ServiceInputs before channel close
Ensure mixed pipelines work: polling Input + ServiceInput in same pipeline
Write tests using mock ServiceInput that pushes metrics asynchronously
verification: All existing tests still pass. New ServiceInput lifecycle tests pass. Mixed input pipeline test passes.
tests: isServiceInput() correctly identifies ServiceInput vs Input
ServiceInput start() called during pipeline startup
ServiceInput pushes metrics asynchronously — output receives them
ServiceInput stop() called during shutdown before channel close
Mixed pipeline: 1 polling input + 1 service input, output sees metrics from both
ServiceInput start() error: logged, pipeline continues with other inputs
metric_batch_size: large batch split into chunks for output.write()

description: Implement Modbus TCP input plugin with register reading, byte order, scaling, and batch reads
steps: Create src/plugins/inputs/modbus.ts
Implement Zod config schema matching PRD §6 ModbusConfigSchema exactly
Implement init(): create modbus-serial TCP client with timeout
Implement gather(): read all configured registers, emit via acc.addFields()
Implement FC01 (coils), FC02 (discrete inputs), FC03 (holding), FC04 (input registers)
Implement multi-register types: uint32, int32, float32 with byte order decoding
Implement scaling (raw * scale + offset) and bit extraction
Implement batch reads: group contiguous same-type registers, respect max_batch_size and max_gap
Implement shared connection mode: use setID() to switch slave_id
Implement Modbus exception handling per PRD §6 table (disable register on 01/02/03, retry on 04/05/06/08/0A/0B)
Implement reconnection on connection loss
Implement close(): disconnect client
Register plugin in plugin registry
Write comprehensive tests
verification: All Modbus tests pass. Existing Phase 1 tests still pass. Config validates via Zod.
tests: Read single holding register (FC03) → correct numeric value
Read coil (FC01) → boolean value
Read input register (FC04) → correct value
Float32 with ABCD byte order → correct float value
Float32 with CDAB byte order → correct float value (word-swapped)
Float32 with BADC byte order → correct float value
Float32 with DCBA byte order → correct float value
Uint32 multi-register → correct value
Scaling: raw * scale + offset produces correct output
Bit extraction: correct bit extracted as boolean
Batch read: contiguous registers read in single request
Gap split: non-contiguous registers split into separate requests
Shared mode: 2 slave IDs on same connection both return correct data
Modbus exception 02 (Illegal Address) → register disabled, others continue
Modbus exception 04 (Slave Failure) → retry next interval, others continue
Connection timeout → error logged, gather completes without crash
Reconnection after connection drop
Config validation: missing controller → error, slave_id out of range → error

description: Integration test: Modbus input → pipeline → mock output
steps: Create test/integration/modbus-pipeline.test.ts
Set up mock Modbus TCP server (or stub modbus-serial)
Configure pipeline with Modbus input + mock output
Verify metrics flow through pipeline with correct names, values, and tags
Verify slave_id appears as tag
Verify global tags applied to Modbus metrics
verification: Integration test passes. Modbus metrics arrive at output with correct structure.
tests: Modbus input → pipeline → output: metrics have correct register names and values
Global tags and slave_id tag present on output metrics
Multiple registers produce multiple fields per gather cycle

description: Implement OPC-UA input plugin — connection, subscription, data types, certificates, reconnection
steps: Create src/plugins/inputs/opcua.ts
Implement Zod config schema matching PRD Appendix D exactly
Implement start(): connect → session → subscription → monitored items
Implement security auto-negotiation (try policies in fallback order)
Implement client certificate generation (self-signed RSA 2048, persist to configured path)
Implement server certificate TOFU (store fingerprint, reject on change)
Implement data type mapping: 22+ OPC-UA types → FieldValue per D.3 table
Implement quality mapping: StatusCode → quality tag (good/uncertain/bad)
Implement timestamp source selection (source/server/gather)
Implement node groups: expand groups into flat node list with inherited defaults
Implement subscription parameters from config
Implement data change filter (deadband)
Implement reconnection with exponential backoff
Implement subscription transfer on reconnect (attempt TransferSubscriptions, fall back to recreate)
Implement namespace URI resolution (nsu= to ns= at connect time)
Implement browse mode: discover nodes, write TOML output file
Implement authentication: anonymous, username/password
Implement stop(): delete subscriptions, close session, close secure channel
Implement error handling per D.7 table
Register plugin in plugin registry
Write comprehensive tests using node-opcua test server
verification: All OPC-UA tests pass. ServiceInput lifecycle works. Certificate workflow verified.
tests: Connect to mock OPC-UA server, read single node → correct metric value
Subscription: monitored item receives data change → metric emitted to accumulator
Data type Boolean → boolean FieldValue
Data type Int32 → number FieldValue
Data type Float → number FieldValue
Data type Double → number FieldValue
Data type String → string FieldValue
Data type DateTime → number (Unix epoch ms)
Quality Good → quality tag 'good', value emitted
Quality Bad → quality tag 'bad', value STILL emitted (not dropped)
Timestamp source=source uses OPC-UA source timestamp
Timestamp source=gather uses local timestamp
Node groups expanded: group defaults inherited, per-node overrides win
Certificate generation: no cert file → auto-generates and persists
TOFU: first connect → fingerprint stored; changed cert → connection rejected
Reconnection: disconnect → backoff → reconnect → subscriptions recreated
Browse mode: discovers nodes, writes TOML snippet to output file
Auth anonymous: connects without credentials
Auth username: connects with username/password
Bad NodeID → error logged, other nodes continue monitoring
Connection refused → retry with backoff, no crash
Auth failure → clear error, no retry (config error)
Deadband absolute: small value changes suppressed
Array type → multiple fields (name[0], name[1], name.length)
Config validation: missing endpoint → error, invalid security policy → error

description: Integration test: OPC-UA input → pipeline → mock output
steps: Create test/integration/opcua-pipeline.test.ts
Set up mock OPC-UA server using node-opcua server module
Configure pipeline with OPC-UA ServiceInput + mock output
Change values on server → verify metrics arrive at output
Verify data type mapping is correct end-to-end
Verify quality tags flow through pipeline
Verify global tags applied to OPC-UA metrics
verification: Integration test passes. OPC-UA subscription metrics flow through full pipeline.
tests: OPC-UA subscription → pipeline → output: value changes produce metrics
Data types preserved through pipeline (number, string, boolean)
Quality tags present on output metrics
Global tags applied to OPC-UA metrics

description: Implement MQTT consumer input plugin — subscribe, parse, reconnect
steps: Choose MQTT library (mqtt.js or alternative) — verify Bun compatibility first
Create src/plugins/inputs/mqtt-consumer.ts
Implement Zod config schema
Implement start(): connect to broker, subscribe to topics
Implement message handler: parse payload → acc.addFields()
Implement JSON payload parsing (flat → fields, nested → dot-notation)
Implement plain string payload (single 'value' field)
Implement topic-to-tag mapping (topic_tag and topic_tags config)
Implement QoS support (0, 1, 2)
Implement TLS configuration
Implement auth: username/password
Implement reconnection with backoff
Implement stop(): unsubscribe, disconnect
Register plugin in plugin registry
Write tests using mock MQTT broker (aedes or similar)
verification: All MQTT tests pass. ServiceInput pattern verified. Reconnection works.
tests: Connect to mock broker, subscribe, receive JSON message → metric with correct fields
JSON payload with multiple fields → single metric with all fields
Nested JSON → dot-notation flattened fields
Plain string payload → metric with single 'value' field
Topic tag extraction: topic 'factory/A/line/1' with topic_tags → correct tags
Wildcard subscription: subscribe to 'sensors/#' → receives from sub-topics
QoS 1: message delivery confirmed
Reconnection: broker disconnect → auto-reconnect → resubscribe
Connection failure → retry with backoff, no crash
Multiple topics: subscribe to 3 topics, metrics from all arrive
Measurement name override from config
Config validation: missing servers → error, missing topics → error

description: Integration test: MQTT consumer → pipeline → mock output
steps: Create test/integration/mqtt-pipeline.test.ts
Set up mock MQTT broker
Configure pipeline with MQTT consumer + mock output
Publish messages to broker → verify metrics arrive at output
Verify topic tags flow through pipeline
verification: Integration test passes. MQTT messages become pipeline metrics.
tests: MQTT message → pipeline → output: JSON fields preserved
Topic tags present on output metrics
Global tags applied to MQTT metrics

description: Implement internal metrics input — agent health and per-plugin stats
steps: Create src/plugins/inputs/internal.ts
Define a StatsCollector interface for pipeline runtime to expose counters
Implement polling Input that reads stats on each gather
Emit collatr.agent.* metrics: uptime, memory, cpu, gather_errors, write_errors
Emit collatr.agent.metrics_gathered, metrics_written, metrics_dropped counters
Emit per-input metrics: collatr.input.gather_time, collatr.input.metrics_count
Emit per-output metrics: collatr.output.write_time, collatr.output.buffer_size
Add StatsCollector to PipelineRuntime (expose counters for internal input)
Register plugin in plugin registry
Write tests
verification: Internal metrics produced with correct names and reasonable values. Pipeline stats accurate.
tests: Internal input produces collatr.agent.uptime metric with positive value
collatr.agent.memory_usage is a positive number (bytes)
collatr.agent.metrics_gathered increases as inputs produce metrics
Per-input gather_time is non-negative (milliseconds)
Metrics have correct collatr.* prefix
Agent hostname/instance tag present
Internal metrics flow through pipeline like normal metrics

description: Integration test: internal metrics + other input → pipeline → output
steps: Create test/integration/internal-pipeline.test.ts
Configure pipeline with internal input + mock polling input + mock output
Verify internal metrics and regular metrics coexist in output
Verify metrics_gathered counter reflects actual input metrics
verification: Internal metrics mixed with regular metrics in output. Counters accurate.
tests: Internal metrics and regular metrics both arrive at output
collatr.agent.metrics_gathered reflects actual metrics produced by other input
